data.title: New York School of Design and Taejae University Announce Landmark Global Partnership to Redefine Creative Leadership
data.description: A collaboration between New York School of Design (NYSD) and Taejae University was officially established this week through the Global Partnership Program. This strategic initiative aims to connect academic theory and advanced design practices. The partnership brings together two prestigious institutions to enhance learning opportunities, with a focus on promoting cross-cultural exchange. The agreement signifies a significant step in fostering international cooperation within the realm of education and design.

data.title: Dogecoin, Shiba Inu At Critical Support—Break These Levels And It Gets Ugly
data.description: Both Dogecoin (CRYPTO: DOGE) and Shiba Inu (CRYPTO: SHIB) are approaching critical support levels following significant declines of 58-60% from their respective highs. Dogecoin has dropped by approximately 60% over a period of five months, falling from $0.23 in October 2025 to its current levels. The price of both cryptocurrencies is currently trapped in descending channels, with negative money flow intensifying. Dogecoin has tested a critical support level at $0.09, as indicated by the Supertrend indicator flashing red at $0.109. A downward trend has been observed in the moving averages above the price, indicating a dominance of sellers across all timeframes. The volume has decreased by 25.5% to $2.86 billion, suggesting a decline in buyer interest during the downtrend. For more details, please visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: /C O R R E C T I O N -- Deloitte/
data.description: Deloitte has been acknowledged as a Leader and Star Performer for the second consecutive year in Everest Group's Private Equity Services PEAK Matrix® Assessment 2026. The news was released on 12-Mar-2026 by Deloitte via PR Newswire. The company has made significant improvements; however, full details were not disclosed.

data.title: Provident Healthcare Partners Advises Hanabusa IVF and Asian Egg Bank on its Partnership with CSG.BIO
data.description: On March 12, 2026, Provident Healthcare Partners disclosed their involvement in the partnership between Hanabusa IVF, Asian Egg Bank, and CSG.BIO. The collaboration marks a significant milestone for Hanabusa IVF, a top-tier private fertility clinic. This move was made possible through the guidance of Provident, a prominent healthcare investment banking firm. The details of the transaction, including the financial aspects, were not disclosed.

data.title: $26B investment into open AI models by Nvidia, introduces Nemotron 3 Super to rival China's AI prowess.
data.description: Nvidia has launched its most advanced open-weight model to date and announced a bold initiative to invest $26 billion over the next five years in developing cutting-edge open models. This move is positioned as a direct competition to China's current dominance in AI technology.

data.title: InventHelp Inventor Develops Water Management Improvement Idea for Walkway Safety (MHO-639)
data.description: In Pittsburgh on March 12, 2026, a home inspector expressed concerns about water being discharged onto walkways from downspouts, leading to safety issues and property damage. The inventor from Nebraska highlighted the need for a solution to address this issue. This innovation aims to tackle the problem by providing a way to divert water away from walkways. This development could potentially prevent accidents and property damage.

data.title: Regulators to ease capital rules; proposals to reduce bank reserve requirements will be released soon.
data.description: Financial regulators, including the Federal Reserve, are preparing to introduce new proposals that would relax certain restrictions on the amount of capital banks are required to maintain as a buffer against potential risks. This initiative aims to revise rules governing capital reserves and promote flexibility within the banking sector. The specific details of the proposals, which are expected to be disclosed shortly, will alter the current regulations in place to ensure financial stability. Stay tuned for updates on this development.

data.title: Voyager Launches Center to Advance U.S. Space and National Security Capabilities
data.description: In Denver, Voyager Technologies (NYSE: VOYG) has unveiled plans for a fresh establishment in Long Beach, California, dubbed “Space Beach,” aimed at amplifying their capability in meeting the rising demand in civil, national security, and commercial space missions. Voyager's President of Space, Defense & National Security, Matt Magaña, expressed, "Our aim in Space Beach is clear - meeting customer needs promptly." The company is hurriedly transitioning from planning to execution, gearing up to serve efficiently.
